rationale: Both titles describe the same PRO-PVI study on suture-mediated femoral venous closure for same-day discharge after PVI. Title X is clean and concise but omits a critical methodological element: the comparative arm and propensity-matched design. For Europace readers, the comparison group and analytical method are highly relevant to assessing evidence quality. Title Y provides this specificity — 'versus standard workflow' and 'propensity-matched cohort' — which substantially increases informativeness and journal fit for a Europace audience that values methodological transparency. The slight clarity trade-off in Y (longer, more complex) is outweighed by the added scientific precision.
loser_weakness: Title X omits the comparator ('versus standard workflow') and the propensity-matched design, making it less informative about the study's comparative nature and analytical rigor. Readers cannot distinguish it from a single-arm feasibility study without reading the abstract.
